questions.question: Write a C program to declare and initialize a pointer to an integer variable and print its memory address. Explain the purpose of the address-of operator (&) used in this program.
What is the difference between the syntax of declaring a single pointer variable (e.g., int *ptr) and a pointer to a pointer variable (e.g., int **ptr)? Provide examples to illustrate your answer.

Write a C program that declares a pointer to an integer variable and stores the value of a variable in the memory location pointed by the pointer. Then, print the value stored at that memory location.
questions.hint: Use pointer variables and memory addressing
